# Claude Spice Harvester — LLM Reference

## What this project is

A single-file macOS menu bar application (Python) that reads Claude Code's local JSONL session files from `~/.claude/` and displays token usage statistics. No network calls at runtime. No server. No account required beyond having Claude Code installed.

The entire application is `claude_spice_harvester.py`. Everything else is packaging, screenshots, and documentation.

## Core technologies

- Python 3.9+
- `rumps` — macOS menu bar framework (pip dependency)
- `pyobjc` / `AppKit` — for colored menu text via NSAttributedString (optional, usually pre-installed on macOS)
- PyInstaller — for building a standalone `.app` (optional, only for packaging)
- HTML/CSS/JS — dashboard rendered as a local `file://` page in the system browser

## File structure

```
claude_spice_harvester.py   — entire app: data layer + HTML template + menu bar UI
build_app.sh                — packages into ClaudeSpiceHarvester.app via PyInstaller
README.md                   — user-facing documentation
docs/
  developer-guide.md        — full developer documentation
  llms.txt                  — this file
screenshots/                — PNG screenshots for README.md
```

## How it works (summary)

1. On launch and every 5 minutes, `load_all_usage()` globs `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` and related paths.
2. Each JSONL file is parsed line-by-line. Lines with `{type: "assistant", message: {usage: {...}}}` or similar shapes are extracted into usage records.
3. `aggregate_records()` buckets records into: all-time totals, today (local date), this week (7 days), and current session (5-hour UTC window).
4. The menu bar title and menu items are updated with formatted token counts.
5. An HTML dashboard is written to `/tmp/claude_spice_harvester.html` and can be opened in the browser.

## Key data shapes

JSONL entries the app recognises (others are silently skipped):

```jsonc
// Format 1 — assistant message with nested usage
{ "type": "assistant", "timestamp": "ISO8601", "message": { "model": "...", "usage": { "input_tokens": N, "output_tokens": N, "cache_read_input_tokens": N, "cache_creation_input_tokens": N } } }

// Format 2 — flat role entry
{ "role": "assistant", "timestamp": "ISO8601", "model": "...", "usage": { ... } }

// Format 3 — cost-keyed entry (older format)
{ "costUSD": 0.004, "inputTokens": N, "outputTokens": N, "cacheReadTokens": N, "cacheWriteTokens": N, "model": "...", "timestamp": "ISO8601" }
```

## Aggregation windows

- **Session**: current 5-hour UTC window (floor(UTC_hour / 5) * 5h boundary). Approximates Claude Pro's reset cycle.
- **Today**: local date equals `date.today()`.
- **Week**: local date within the last 7 days inclusive.
- **Totals**: all records regardless of date.

## Cost estimation

Cost is estimated (not actual billing) using `estimate_cost()`:

| Model substring | Input $/M | Output $/M | Cache read $/M | Cache write $/M |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| claude-opus | 15.00 | 75.00 | 1.50 | 3.75 |
| claude-sonnet | 3.00 | 15.00 | 0.30 | 3.75 |
| claude-haiku | 0.25 | 1.25 | 0.03 | 0.30 |
| (fallback) | 3.00 | 15.00 | 0.30 | 3.75 |

Claude Code subscribers on Pro/Max pay a flat subscription. These figures do not reflect actual billing.

## Themes

Three themes are defined in the `THEMES` dict: `arrakis` (default, gold Dune aesthetic), `caladan` (ocean blue), `giedi_prime` (industrial grey/red). The active theme is persisted to `~/Library/Application Support/ClaudeSpiceHarvester/config.json`.

Each theme defines:
- `menu_colors` — per-item (R, G, B) tuples for NSAttributedString
- `css_vars` — CSS custom properties for the HTML dashboard
- `bg_gradients`, `particles` — visual decoration
- `title`, `tagline`, `ornament`, `dividers`, `footer`, `gauge_label`, `stat_labels` — copy strings

## Critical implementation notes for LLMs

**HTML template uses `str.replace()`, never `.format()`.**
`HTML_TEMPLATE` is a raw string containing CSS `{ }` blocks. Using `.format()` will raise a `KeyError` on CSS property declarations. All substitutions must use the `substitutions` dict with explicit `html.replace(k, v)` calls.

**`_paint()` must be called after every `.title` assignment.**
Setting `item.title = "..."` on a `rumps.MenuItem` clears the `NSAttributedString`. `_paint(item, rgb)` must be called again to restore color. Every stat item title update in `refresh_data()` is followed by a `_paint()` call.

**Non-interactive items use `callback=lambda _: None`, not `callback=None`.**
`rumps.MenuItem(callback=None)` is grayed out by macOS and cannot be colored. Stat display items need `callback=lambda _: None` to remain enabled (and thus colorable) while still doing nothing on click.

**Session window is a heuristic.**
The 5-hour UTC boundary approximation does not guarantee alignment with Anthropic's server-side session accounting.

**No public API for individual usage data.**
Anthropic's Usage & Cost Admin API requires an organization account. For personal Pro/Max subscriptions, local JSONL files are the only available data source.

## Entry points

```python
# Normal run
python3 claude_spice_harvester.py

# Screenshot mode (requires pyobjc) — opens menu, captures it, quits
python3 claude_spice_harvester.py --screenshot output.png
```

## What to change for common tasks

| Task | Where |
|---|---|
| Change refresh interval | `rumps.Timer(self._auto_refresh, 300)` in `__init__` — change `300` |
| Update token pricing | `pricing` dict in `estimate_cost()` |
| Add a new theme | Add entry to `THEMES` dict — submenu is built dynamically |
| Add a new stat to the dashboard | Extract in `extract_usage_from_entries()`, accumulate in `aggregate_records()`, add placeholder to `HTML_TEMPLATE`, substitute in `build_html()` |
| Change menu bar icon | `"🏜"` string in `ClaudeSpiceHarvesterApp.__init__` super().__init__ call |
